```yaml
  config.yaml: |
    version: v1
    mig-configs:
      mixed-workload:
        - device-filter: ["0x20B210DE"]
          devices: [0,1,2,3]
          mig-enabled: true
          mig-devices:
            "3g.40gb": 1
            "1g.10gb": 4
        - device-filter: ["0x20B210DE"]
          devices: [4,5,6,7]
          mig-enabled: false
```
This gives: 4Г— 3g.40gb + 16Г— 1g.10gb MIG slices + 4 full GPUs.

exercises.title: PyTorchJob for Distributed Training
exercises.description:
Write a Kubeflow PyTorchJob CRD that launches a distributed fine-tuning
job with 1 Master and 3 Workers. Each replica uses the image
myregistry/llm-finetune:v2.0, requests 8 GPUs, 64 CPUs, and 512Gi memory.
Include NCCL environment variables, mount a PVC 'training-data' at /data,
and use torchrun with elastic training support.
exercises.starter_code:
# pytorchjob-finetune.yaml
apiVersion: kubeflow.org/v1
kind: PyTorchJob
metadata:
  name: llm-finetune
  namespace: ml-training
spec:
  # TODO: Add elastic policy (min 2, max 8 replicas)

  pytorchReplicaSpecs:
    # TODO: Master spec
    #   - 1 replica
    #   - image: myregistry/llm-finetune:v2.0
    #   - command: torchrun with --nproc_per_node=8
    #   - env: NCCL_DEBUG=INFO, NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth0
    #   - resources: 64 CPU, 512Gi, 8 GPU
    #   - volumeMount: training-data at /data

    # TODO: Worker spec (3 replicas, same config as Master)
hints: elasticPolicy goes under spec, before pytorchReplicaSpecs.
Both Master and Worker need identical container specs.
The Training Operator auto-sets MASTER_ADDR and MASTER_PORT.
exercises.solution:
```yaml
apiVersion: kubeflow.org/v1
kind: PyTorchJob
metadata:
  name: llm-finetune
  namespace: ml-training
spec:
  elasticPolicy:
    minReplicas: 2
    maxReplicas: 8
    rdzvBackend: c10d
  pytorchReplicaSpecs:
    Master:
      replicas: 1
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: pytorch
              image: myregistry/llm-finetune:v2.0
              command:
                - torchrun
                - --nproc_per_node=8
                - --nnodes=$(WORLD_SIZE)
                - train.py
                - --bf16
              env:
                - name: NCCL_DEBUG
                  value: "INFO"
                - name: NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME
                  value: "eth0"
              resources:
                limits:
                  cpu: "64"
                  memory: "512Gi"
                  nvidia.com/gpu: 8
              volumeMounts:
                - name: data
                  mountPath: /data
          volumes:
            - name: data
              persistentVolumeClaim:
                claimName: training-data
    Worker:
      replicas: 3
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: pytorch
              image: myregistry/llm-finetune:v2.0
              command:
                - torchrun
                - --nproc_per_node=8
                - --nnodes=$(WORLD_SIZE)
                - train.py
                - --bf16
              env:
                - name: NCCL_DEBUG
                  value: "INFO"
                - name: NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME
                  value: "eth0"
              resources:
                limits:
                  cpu: "64"
                  memory: "512Gi"
                  nvidia.com/gpu: 8
              volumeMounts:
                - name: data
                  mountPath: /data
          volumes:
            - name: data
              persistentVolumeClaim:
                claimName: training-data
```

exercises.title: GPU Monitoring with DCGM and Prometheus
exercises.description:
Write: (1) a DCGM Exporter ConfigMap defining custom metrics to collect
(GPU utilization, memory used/free, temperature, power); (2) a ServiceMonitor
for Prometheus to scrape the DCGM exporter; (3) a PrometheusRule with alerts
for GPU temperature > 85В°C and GPU memory > 90% usage.
exercises.starter_code:
# TODO: DCGM Exporter ConfigMap with metrics list
#   Include: GPU_UTIL, FB_USED, FB_FREE, GPU_TEMP, POWER_USAGE

---
# TODO: ServiceMonitor for Prometheus scraping
#   Target the dcgm-exporter service in gpu-operator namespace
#   Scrape interval: 15s

---
# TODO: PrometheusRule with alerts:
#   1. GPUTemperatureHigh: GPU temp > 85В°C for 5m
#   2. GPUMemoryHigh: GPU memory usage > 90% for 10m
hints: DCGM metric names use the DCGM_FI_DEV_ prefix.
ServiceMonitor uses spec.selector to find the target service.
PrometheusRule uses PromQL expressions in the expr field.
exercises.solution:
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: dcgm-exporter-metrics
  namespace: gpu-operator
data:
  dcgm-metrics.csv: |
    DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL, gauge, GPU utilization (%)
    DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED, gauge, Framebuffer memory used (MB)
    DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_FREE, gauge, Framebuffer memory free (MB)
    DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP, gauge, GPU temperature (C)
    DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE, gauge, Power usage (W)
---
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: dcgm-exporter
  namespace: gpu-operator
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nvidia-dcgm-exporter
  endpoints:
    - port: metrics
      interval: 15s
---
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: PrometheusRule
metadata:
  name: gpu-alerts
  namespace: gpu-operator
spec:
  groups:
    - name: gpu.rules
      rules:
        - alert: GPUTemperatureHigh
          expr: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP > 85
          for: 5m
          labels:
            severity: warning
          annotations:
            summary: "GPU temperature exceeds 85В°C"
        - alert: GPUMemoryHigh
          expr: >
            DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED /
            (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED + DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_FREE) * 100 > 90
          for: 10m
          labels:
            severity: warning
          annotations:
            summary: "GPU memory usage exceeds 90%"
```

exercises.title: Time-Slicing Configuration for Development Cluster
exercises.description:
Write the complete configuration to enable 4-way GPU time-slicing on a
Kubernetes cluster: (1) the device plugin ConfigMap with time-slicing
settings; (2) the Helm values for reinstalling the NVIDIA device plugin
with this config; (3) a sample Pod that requests one time-sliced GPU share;
(4) a command to verify the node now advertises 4Г— the physical GPU count.
exercises.starter_code:
# Step 1: TODO вҖ” ConfigMap for time-slicing (4 replicas per GPU)

---
# Step 2: TODO вҖ” Helm install command for NVIDIA device plugin
#   (as a comment showing the helm install command)

---
# Step 3: TODO вҖ” Pod requesting one time-sliced share

---
# Step 4: TODO вҖ” Verification command (as a comment)
hints: The ConfigMap goes in kube-system or gpu-operator namespace.
With replicas: 4, an 8-GPU node advertises 32 nvidia.com/gpu.
Pods still request nvidia.com/gpu: 1 вҖ” they get a time-slice.
exercises.solution:
```yaml
# Step 1: ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: device-plugin-config
  namespace: gpu-operator
data:
  config.yaml: |
    version: v1
    sharing:
      timeSlicing:
        renameByDefault: false
        failRequestsGreaterThanOne: false
        resources:
          - name: nvidia.com/gpu
            replicas: 4
---
# Step 2: Helm install
# helm upgrade --install nvidia-device-plugin nvdp/nvidia-device-plugin \
#   --namespace gpu-operator \
#   --set config.name=device-plugin-config \
#   --set gfd.enabled=true
---
# Step 3: Pod
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: dev-notebook
spec:
  containers:
    - name: notebook
      image: quay.io/jupyter/pytorch-notebook:cuda12-latest
      resources:
        limits:
          nvidia.com/gpu: 1
---
# Step 4: Verify (8 physical GPUs Г— 4 = 32 advertised)
# kubectl get node gpu-node-01 -o jsonpath='{.status.capacity.nvidia\.com/gpu}'
# Expected: 32
```

lessons.title: Helm Charts & Deployments
objectives: Understand Helm concepts: charts, values, templates, releases, and repositories
Create custom Helm charts with proper structure and best practices
Write Go templates with built-in functions, conditionals, and loops
Deploy ML platforms (JupyterHub, MLflow, Kubeflow) with Helm
Manage multiple releases with Helmfile
lessons.content_md:
# Helm Charts & Deployments

## 1. Why Helm?

Deploying an ML platform on Kubernetes requires dozens of
interdependent YAML manifests: Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps,
Secrets, PVCs, RBAC, Ingress. **Helm** is the package manager for
Kubernetes вҖ” it bundles manifests into reusable, version-controlled,
parameterized **charts**.

```
Without Helm              With Helm
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ deployment.yaml   в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ helm install       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ service.yaml      в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   mlflow           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ configmap.yaml    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   bitnami/mlflow   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ secret.yaml       в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   --values prod.yamlв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ pvc.yaml          в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ ingress.yaml      в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ вҶ’ creates all      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ rbac.yaml         в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   resources with   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ hpa.yaml          в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   proper config    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ networkpolicy.yamlв”Ӯ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
в”Ӯ (apply each one)  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Key benefits:
- **Templating**: parameterize manifests with `values.yaml`
- **Versioning**: track releases, rollback to previous versions
- **Dependencies**: manage sub-charts and cross-chart dependencies
- **Ecosystem**: thousands of community charts for popular software

## 2. Helm Concepts

| Concept       | Description                                      |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| **Chart**     | A package of Kubernetes manifests + metadata     |
| **Values**    | Configuration parameters (values.yaml)           |
| **Template**  | Manifest with Go template directives             |
| **Release**   | An installed instance of a chart                 |
| **Repository**| A hosted collection of charts                    |
| **Revision**  | A version of a release (for rollback)            |

```bash
# Key Helm commands
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
helm search repo mlflow
helm install my-mlflow bitnami/mlflow --values custom.yaml
helm upgrade my-mlflow bitnami/mlflow --values custom.yaml
helm rollback my-mlflow 1
helm uninstall my-mlflow
helm list --all-namespaces
helm history my-mlflow
```

:::interactive{component="TerminalSimulator" config='{"preset":"helm-basics"}'}

## 3. Chart Structure

```
my-ml-platform/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Chart.yaml            # Chart metadata (name, version, deps)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Chart.lock            # Locked dependency versions
в”ңв”Җв”Җ values.yaml           # Default configuration values
в”ңв”Җв”Җ values.schema.json    # JSON Schema for values validation
в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ _helpers.tpl      # Reusable named templates
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ deployment.yaml   # Deployment template
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ service.yaml      # Service template
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ configmap.yaml    # ConfigMap template
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ secret.yaml       # Secret template
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ pvc.yaml          # PersistentVolumeClaim template
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ingress.yaml      # Ingress template (optional)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ hpa.yaml          # HorizontalPodAutoscaler (optional)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ serviceaccount.yaml
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ NOTES.txt         # Post-install usage instructions
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ tests/
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ test-connection.yaml
в”ңв”Җв”Җ charts/               # Sub-chart dependencies
в””в”Җв”Җ .helmignore           # Files to ignore when packaging
```

### 3.1 Chart.yaml

```yaml
apiVersion: v2
name: ml-inference
description: A Helm chart for deploying ML inference services
type: application
version: 1.2.0           # Chart version
appVersion: "2.1.0"      # Application version
keywords:
  - machine-learning
  - inference
  - gpu
maintainers:
  - name: ML Platform Team
    email: ml-platform@company.com
dependencies:
  - name: redis
    version: "18.x.x"
    repository: "https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami"
    condition: redis.enabled
  - name: postgresql
    version: "14.x.x"
    repository: "https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami"
    condition: postgresql.enabled
```

### 3.2 values.yaml

```yaml
# values.yaml вҖ” Default configuration
replicaCount: 2

image:
  repository: myregistry/triton-server
  tag: "24.01-py3"
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    http: 8000
    grpc: 8001
    metrics: 8002

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "4"
    memory: "16Gi"
  limits:
    cpu: "8"
    memory: "32Gi"
    nvidia.com/gpu: 1

modelRepository:
  storageType: pvc       # pvc, s3, or gcs
  pvc:
    name: model-store
    mountPath: /models
  s3:
    bucket: ""
    region: ""

autoscaling:
  enabled: true
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  targetCPUUtilization: 70
  targetGPUUtilization: 70

ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: nginx
  hosts:
    - host: inference.example.com
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: Prefix

redis:
  enabled: true

postgresql:
  enabled: false
```

## 4. Template Language

Helm uses Go's `text/template` engine with Sprig functions.

### 4.1 Basic Templating

```yaml
# templates/deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "ml-inference.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "ml-inference.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  {{- if not .Values.autoscaling.enabled }}
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}
  {{- end }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      {{- include "ml-inference.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        {{- include "ml-inference.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
      annotations:
        checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/configmap.yaml") . | sha256sum }}
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}
          image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}"
          imagePullPolicy: {{ .Values.image.pullPolicy }}
          ports:
            {{- range $name, $port := .Values.service.ports }}
            - name: {{ $name }}
              containerPort: {{ $port }}
              protocol: TCP
            {{- end }}
          resources:
            {{- toYaml .Values.resources | nindent 12 }}
          {{- if eq .Values.modelRepository.storageType "pvc" }}
          volumeMounts:
            - name: model-store
              mountPath: {{ .Values.modelRepository.pvc.mountPath }}
          {{- end }}
      {{- if eq .Values.modelRepository.storageType "pvc" }}
      volumes:
        - name: model-store
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{ .Values.modelRepository.pvc.name }}
      {{- end }}
```

### 4.2 Helper Templates (_helpers.tpl)

```yaml
# templates/_helpers.tpl

{{/*
Expand the name of the chart.
*/}}
{{- define "ml-inference.name" -}}
{{- default .Chart.Name .Values.nameOverride | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- end }}

{{/*
Create a fully qualified app name.
*/}}
{{- define "ml-inference.fullname" -}}
{{- if .Values.fullnameOverride }}
{{- .Values.fullnameOverride | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- else }}
{{- $name := default .Chart.Name .Values.nameOverride }}
{{- if contains $name .Release.Name }}
{{- .Release.Name | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- else }}
{{- printf "%s-%s" .Release.Name $name | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- end }}
{{- end }}
{{- end }}

{{/*
Common labels
*/}}
{{- define "ml-inference.labels" -}}
helm.sh/chart: {{ include "ml-inference.chart" . }}
{{ include "ml-inference.selectorLabels" . }}
app.kubernetes.io/version: {{ .Chart.AppVersion | quote }}
app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: {{ .Release.Service }}
{{- end }}

{{/*
Selector labels
*/}}
{{- define "ml-inference.selectorLabels" -}}
app.kubernetes.io/name: {{ include "ml-inference.name" . }}
app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{ .Release.Name }}
{{- end }}

{{/*
Chart label
*/}}
{{- define "ml-inference.chart" -}}
{{- printf "%s-%s" .Chart.Name .Chart.Version | replace "+" "_" | trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-" }}
{{- end }}
```

### 4.3 Key Template Functions

| Function        | Example                                      | Result            |
|-----------------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| `default`       | `{{ default "nginx" .Values.class }}`        | "nginx" if empty  |
| `quote`         | `{{ .Values.tag \| quote }}`                  | `"v1.0"`          |
| `upper`/`lower` | `{{ .Values.env \| upper }}`                  | `PRODUCTION`      |
| `trunc`         | `{{ .name \| trunc 63 }}`                     | Limit to 63 chars |
| `trimSuffix`    | `{{ .name \| trimSuffix "-" }}`               | Remove trailing - |
| `nindent`       | `{{ toYaml .data \| nindent 4 }}`             | Indent 4 spaces   |
| `toYaml`        | `{{ toYaml .Values.resources }}`              | YAML output       |
| `sha256sum`     | `{{ .data \| sha256sum }}`                    | SHA-256 hash      |
| `b64enc`        | `{{ .secret \| b64enc }}`                     | Base64 encode     |
| `required`      | `{{ required "msg" .Values.key }}`            | Fail if missing   |
| `ternary`       | `{{ ternary "a" "b" .Values.flag }}`          | If/else shorthand |

### 4.4 Conditionals and Loops

```yaml
# Conditional inclusion
{{- if .Values.ingress.enabled }}
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
# ...
{{- end }}

# Loops
{{- range .Values.ingress.hosts }}
- host: {{ .host | quote }}
  http:
    paths:
      {{- range .paths }}
      - path: {{ .path }}
        pathType: {{ .pathType }}
      {{- end }}
{{- end }}

# Named range with index
{{- range $key, $value := .Values.env }}
- name: {{ $key }}
  value: {{ $value | quote }}
{{- end }}
```

## 5. Deploying ML Platforms with Helm

### 5.1 JupyterHub

```bash
helm repo add jupyterhub https://hub.jupyter.org/helm-chart/
helm repo update

helm install jhub jupyterhub/jupyterhub \
    --namespace jupyter \
    --create-namespace \
    --values jupyterhub-values.yaml
```

```yaml
# jupyterhub-values.yaml
proxy:
  service:
    type: LoadBalancer
singleuser:
  profileList:
    - display_name: "GPU Data Science"
      description: "PyTorch + CUDA with 1 GPU"
      kubespawner_override:
        image: quay.io/jupyter/pytorch-notebook:cuda12-latest
        extra_resource_limits:
          nvidia.com/gpu: "1"
        extra_resource_guarantees:
          cpu: "4"
          memory: "16Gi"
    - display_name: "CPU Data Science"
      description: "Standard data science environment"
      default: true
      kubespawner_override:
        image: quay.io/jupyter/datascience-notebook:latest
  storage:
    capacity: 50Gi
    storageClass: fast-nvme
hub:
  config:
    Authenticator:
      admin_users: ["admin"]
```

### 5.2 MLflow

```bash
helm repo add community-charts https://community-charts.github.io/helm-charts
helm install mlflow community-charts/mlflow \
    --namespace mlflow \
    --create-namespace \
    --set backendStore.postgres.enabled=true \
    --set backendStore.postgres.host=postgres.mlflow.svc \
    --set artifactRoot.s3.enabled=true \
    --set artifactRoot.s3.bucket=mlflow-artifacts
```

### 5.3 Kubeflow Training Operator

```bash
kubectl apply -k "github.com/kubeflow/training-operator/manifests/overlays/standalone"
```

## 6. Creating Custom Charts for HPC

### 6.1 Scaffold and Customize

```bash
helm create hpc-job-launcher
```

### 6.2 HPC-Specific Templates

```yaml
# templates/job.yaml вҖ” for batch HPC workloads
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: {{ include "hpc-job-launcher.fullname" . }}-{{ .Release.Revision }}
  labels:
    {{- include "hpc-job-launcher.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  completions: {{ .Values.job.completions }}
  parallelism: {{ .Values.job.parallelism }}
  backoffLimit: {{ .Values.job.backoffLimit }}
  ttlSecondsAfterFinished: {{ .Values.job.ttlSeconds }}
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      {{- with .Values.nodeSelector }}
      nodeSelector:
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
      {{- end }}
      {{- with .Values.tolerations }}
      tolerations:
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
      {{- end }}
      containers:
        - name: hpc-worker
          image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}"
          command: {{ toYaml .Values.job.command | nindent 12 }}
          resources:
            {{- toYaml .Values.resources | nindent 12 }}
          {{- if .Values.volumes.data.enabled }}
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: {{ .Values.volumes.data.mountPath }}
              readOnly: {{ .Values.volumes.data.readOnly }}
          {{- end }}
      {{- if .Values.volumes.data.enabled }}
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{ .Values.volumes.data.pvcName }}
      {{- end }}
```

## 7. Helmfile for Managing Multiple Releases

When deploying an entire ML platform, you need multiple Helm charts
coordinated together. **Helmfile** manages this declaratively:

```yaml
# helmfile.yaml
repositories:
  - name: nvidia
    url: https://helm.ngc.nvidia.com/nvidia
  - name: jupyterhub
    url: https://hub.jupyter.org/helm-chart/
  - name: prometheus
    url: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

releases:
  - name: gpu-operator
    namespace: gpu-operator
    chart: nvidia/gpu-operator
    version: "24.3.0"
    values:
      - gpu-operator-values.yaml

  - name: prometheus
    namespace: monitoring
    chart: prometheus/kube-prometheus-stack
    version: "58.x.x"
    values:
      - prometheus-values.yaml

  - name: jupyterhub
    namespace: jupyter
    chart: jupyterhub/jupyterhub
    version: "3.3.x"
    values:
      - jupyterhub-values.yaml
    needs:
      - gpu-operator/gpu-operator

  - name: mlflow
    namespace: mlflow
    chart: community-charts/mlflow
    version: "0.10.x"
    values:
      - mlflow-values.yaml
```

```bash
# Apply all releases
helmfile apply

# Diff before applying (shows what will change)
helmfile diff

# Sync specific releases
helmfile -l name=jupyterhub apply

# Destroy everything
helmfile destroy
```

:::interactive{component="PipelineBuilder" config='{"preset":"cicd"}'}

## 8. Best Practices

### 8.1 Chart Development

```bash
# Lint your chart
helm lint ./my-chart

# Render templates locally (without installing)
helm template my-release ./my-chart --values test-values.yaml

# Dry-run against the cluster
helm install my-release ./my-chart --dry-run --debug

# Package for distribution
helm package ./my-chart
```

### 8.2 Production Checklist

- [ ] Use `required` for mandatory values
- [ ] Set default resource requests and limits
- [ ] Include health check probes in Deployments
- [ ] Add NOTES.txt with post-install instructions
- [ ] Include chart tests (`templates/tests/`)
- [ ] Use `.helmignore` to exclude unnecessary files
- [ ] Pin dependency versions in Chart.lock
- [ ] Use `values.schema.json` for validation

exercises.title: Create a Helm Chart values.yaml
exercises.description:
Write a complete values.yaml for a Helm chart that deploys an ML inference
server. Include settings for: image (nvcr.io/nvidia/tritonserver:24.01-py3),
3 replicas, GPU resource limits (1 GPU, 8 CPU, 32Gi memory), service ports
(http: 8000, grpc: 8001, metrics: 8002), PVC-based model repository at
/models, autoscaling (min 2, max 10), and an optional ingress.
exercises.starter_code:
# values.yaml for ML Inference Helm Chart

# TODO: Image configuration
#   repository, tag, pullPolicy

# TODO: Replica count

# TODO: Service configuration
#   type: ClusterIP
#   ports: http (8000), grpc (8001), metrics (8002)

# TODO: Resource requests and limits
#   CPU, memory, GPU

# TODO: Model repository configuration
#   PVC-based, mount at /models

# TODO: Autoscaling configuration
#   enabled, min/max replicas, target utilization

# TODO: Ingress configuration (disabled by default)
hints: Group related settings into nested objects (image, service, resources).
Include both requests and limits for CPU and memory.
Make ingress disabled by default with 'enabled: false'.
exercises.solution:
```yaml
replicaCount: 3

image:
  repository: nvcr.io/nvidia/tritonserver
  tag: "24.01-py3"
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    http: 8000
    grpc: 8001
    metrics: 8002

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "8"
    memory: "32Gi"
  limits:
    cpu: "8"
    memory: "32Gi"
    nvidia.com/gpu: 1

modelRepository:
  storageType: pvc
  pvc:
    name: model-store
    mountPath: /models

autoscaling:
  enabled: true
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  targetCPUUtilization: 70

ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: nginx
  hosts:
    - host: inference.example.com
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: Prefix
```

exercises.title: Write Helm Template Helpers
exercises.description:
Write a _helpers.tpl file for a chart named 'ml-platform'. Include named
templates for: (1) chart name (truncated to 63 chars); (2) fully qualified
name using release name; (3) common labels (chart, selector labels, version,
managed-by); (4) selector labels (name, instance); (5) a GPU resources
template that conditionally includes GPU limits.
hints: Use 'define' and 'end' to create named templates.
Use trunc 63 and trimSuffix '-' for K8s name compliance.
Use {{- if .Values.gpu.enabled }} for conditional GPU resources.

exercises.title: Deploy JupyterHub with Custom Values
exercises.description:
Write a JupyterHub Helm values file that configures: (1) a LoadBalancer
proxy service; (2) two singleuser profiles вҖ” "GPU Data Science" with 1 GPU
and the pytorch-notebook image, and "CPU Data Science" (default) with the
datascience-notebook image; (3) 50Gi persistent storage per user with the
fast-nvme StorageClass; (4) admin users list. Then show the helm install
command.
exercises.starter_code:
# jupyterhub-values.yaml

# TODO: Proxy service configuration (LoadBalancer)

# TODO: Single user configuration
#   - Profile list with GPU and CPU options
#   - GPU profile: pytorch-notebook with cuda12, 1 GPU, 4 CPU, 16Gi
#   - CPU profile: datascience-notebook (default)

# TODO: User storage (50Gi, fast-nvme StorageClass)

# TODO: Hub configuration with admin users

# ---
# TODO: Helm install command (as a comment)
hints: Use singleuser.profileList for multiple environment options.
GPU resources go under kubespawner_override.extra_resource_limits.
Storage is configured under singleuser.storage.
exercises.solution:
```yaml
# jupyterhub-values.yaml
proxy:
  service:
    type: LoadBalancer

singleuser:
  profileList:
    - display_name: "GPU Data Science"
      description: "PyTorch + CUDA with 1 NVIDIA GPU"
      kubespawner_override:
        image: quay.io/jupyter/pytorch-notebook:cuda12-latest
        extra_resource_limits:
          nvidia.com/gpu: "1"
        extra_resource_guarantees:
          cpu: "4"
          memory: "16Gi"
    - display_name: "CPU Data Science"
      description: "Standard data science environment"
      default: true
      kubespawner_override:
        image: quay.io/jupyter/datascience-notebook:latest
        extra_resource_guarantees:
          cpu: "2"
          memory: "8Gi"
  storage:
    capacity: 50Gi
    storageClass: fast-nvme

hub:
  config:
    Authenticator:
      admin_users:
        - admin
        - ml-lead

# Install command:
# helm install jhub jupyterhub/jupyterhub \
#   --namespace jupyter --create-namespace \
#   --values jupyterhub-values.yaml
```

exercises.title: Helmfile for ML Platform Stack
exercises.description:
Write a Helmfile that deploys a complete ML platform with four releases:
(1) NVIDIA GPU Operator from nvidia repo; (2) Prometheus monitoring stack
from prometheus-community; (3) JupyterHub from jupyterhub repo (depends on
GPU operator); (4) MLflow from community-charts. Each release should
reference its own values file and specify the correct namespace.
exercises.starter_code:
# helmfile.yaml вҖ” ML Platform Stack

# TODO: Define Helm repositories
#   nvidia, jupyterhub, prometheus-community, community-charts

# TODO: Define releases
#   1. gpu-operator (namespace: gpu-operator)
#   2. prometheus (namespace: monitoring)
#   3. jupyterhub (namespace: jupyter, depends on gpu-operator)
#   4. mlflow (namespace: mlflow)
hints: Use 'needs:' to declare dependencies between releases.
Each release needs: name, namespace, chart, version, and values.
Repository URLs: nvidia=https://helm.ngc.nvidia.com/nvidia
exercises.solution:
```yaml
repositories:
  - name: nvidia
    url: https://helm.ngc.nvidia.com/nvidia
  - name: jupyterhub
    url: https://hub.jupyter.org/helm-chart/
  - name: prometheus-community
    url: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
  - name: community-charts
    url: https://community-charts.github.io/helm-charts

releases:
  - name: gpu-operator
    namespace: gpu-operator
    chart: nvidia/gpu-operator
    version: "24.3.0"
    values:
      - values/gpu-operator-values.yaml

  - name: prometheus
    namespace: monitoring
    chart: prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack
    version: "58.7.2"
    values:
      - values/prometheus-values.yaml

  - name: jupyterhub
    namespace: jupyter
    chart: jupyterhub/jupyterhub
    version: "3.3.7"
    values:
      - values/jupyterhub-values.yaml
    needs:
      - gpu-operator/gpu-operator

  - name: mlflow
    namespace: mlflow
    chart: community-charts/mlflow
    version: "0.10.0"
    values:
      - values/mlflow-values.yaml
```
